meta.tagline: The AI-native operating system for group travel, touring, and events.
stack: Supabase: Postgres + Auth + Realtime + Storage + ~96 Edge Functions (Deno)
Gemini text via lovable-concierge (38 tools / 18 query classes) + Vertex AI Live voice
Capacitor 8 (web + PWA + iOS wrapper — NOT React Native)
Sentry + PostHog; Vercel (frontend) + Render (OG unfurl proxy)
sources: Static analysis: file counts, LOC ranking, import graph, live knip scan (2026-06-22 refresh)
AHS remediation commits through 2026-05-30 (export/import hardening, lazy chart/export boundaries, edge secret defaults)
App Store readiness pass through 2026-06-04 (UGC block UI, iOS billing steering, signup terms, dead-scaffold + dead AASA endpoint removal)

scores.label: Overall Codebase Health
scores.rationale: Mature, feature-complete product with strong repo memory, remediation tracking, and several recent hardening slices landed. Enterprise security/identity layer is now solid: immutable + hash-chained admin audit log, append-only security_audit_log, a DB-backed audited super_admins table, and a buyer-facing evidence packet (SECURITY_MODEL.md, ENTERPRISE_SECURITY_READINESS/AUDIT docs). Export privacy paths emit table-level manifests and hard-fail required omissions; trip PDF export membership policy is tested; file AI parsing short-circuits cached replays; Gmail imports distinguish completed/completed_partial/failed; chart and org-chart export dependencies are behind lazy/user-intent boundaries. Still pulled down by concentrated god files (Index, demoModeService, useTripTasks, useAuth, TripChat, useStreamTripChat), hundreds of production `any` usages, a large mock/demo data surface, and dead-code findings that require careful triage.

scores.label: Architecture Clarity
scores.rationale: Feature-module convention (src/features/*), a single src/store/, and the new AHS-01..AHS-19 remediation registry make the critical path clearer. Recent extraction of chart panels, export manifest policy, trip export authorization policy, file-parser idempotency helpers, and Gmail terminal-status policy moved several rules out of long handlers/components. Clarity is still weakened by a 350+ component src/components/ surface, mock/demo data living near service code, and several 1k+ LOC route/hook/chat modules that still mix orchestration, data shaping, and UI.

scores.label: Maintainability
scores.rationale: Maintainability improved from focused policy extraction and tests: export-user-data manifest logic, export-trip authorization/default sections, file-ai-parser idempotency, and Gmail terminal status now live in small tested helpers. The main drag remains unchanged and has grown slightly: Index.tsx (~1.5k), demoModeService.ts (~1.3k), useTripTasks.ts (~1.3k), useAuth.tsx (~1.3k), TripChat.tsx (~1.3k), useStreamTripChat.ts, plus two newer 1k+ LOC entrants — src/lib/blog.ts (~1.5k, static blog-content registry) and src/utils/exportPdfClient.ts (~1.2k, jsPDF client export) — alongside generated Supabase types (5.8k) and large mock datasets. Production `any` usage has crept from ~390 to ~450 and the baseline ESLint warning count still adds daily DX drag.

scores.label: Scalability
scores.rationale: Supabase + edge functions + RLS remain horizontally scalable, and recent client changes moved Recharts chart panels and html2canvas org-chart export behind lazy/user-intent boundaries. The bundle ceiling is still real: exceljs, pdf/jsPDF/html2canvas, native, stream-chat, and chart chunks remain large, and Stream/client boot plus spreadsheet import parsing still need tighter intent boundaries and CI budgets for mobile first-paint protection.

scores.label: Security / Posture
scores.rationale: Security posture improved materially. Tenant/identity layer is now hardened end-to-end: admin_audit_logs is append-only (UPDATE/DELETE/TRUNCATE blocked for all roles incl. service_role) AND tamper-evident via a sha256 hash chain (prev_hash/event_hash ordered by a monotonic seq, with verify_admin_audit_chain()); security_audit_log is append-only and now actually written to (the _shared/security.ts logSecurityEvent helper previously inserted nonexistent columns and silently no-opped — fixed); super-admin access moved from a hardcoded email allowlist to a DB-backed super_admins table with grant/revoke functions and an audit trail; capability-token issuance (Stream/LiveKit) is audit-logged via a shared logSecurityEvent helper; trip-membership checks in edge functions are centralized in _shared/verifyTripMembership.ts (canonical is_trip_member RPC). Earlier slices still hold: export manifests fail closed, trip-export auth is tested, file-ai-parser is idempotent, Gmail terminal states persist. Buyer-facing evidence now exists (SECURITY_MODEL.md, docs/ENTERPRISE_SECURITY_READINESS.md, docs/ENTERPRISE_SECURITY_AUDIT_2026-06.md). Remaining risks: external WORM audit-log sink not yet built (in-DB tamper-evidence only), service-role seed functions, live Supabase integration coverage for role matrices, and continued RLS-drift vigilance.

scores.label: Test Coverage Confidence
scores.rationale: Test confidence improved with new focused Vitest coverage for edge-function secret defaults, export manifests, trip export authorization/default sections, file parser idempotency helpers, and Gmail import terminal status. Coverage remains broad but thin on the highest-risk live paths: chat reconnect/backfill, read-receipt write amplification, export/import live Supabase row-count/replay tests, calendar-sync idempotency, DB-level concurrency, and authenticated Stream/Gmail staging E2E.

scores.label: Dead Code Risk (higher = worse)
scores.rationale: Dead-code risk improved modestly: the App Store readiness pass removed dead scaffold and a dead AASA endpoint, the live knip scan now reports 0 unused dependencies (down from 8) and unused files fell from 285 to 210, and no new scratch cleanup scripts were introduced. But no large knip triage batch of unused exports/types has landed — knip still reports ~210 unused files, 386 unused exports, and 433 unused exported types, with many likely false positives from Vercel API routes, Remotion compositions, and scripts. claude-progress.txt is an intentional agent breadcrumb file, not a scratch artifact. Risk stays elevated until export/type findings are classified and deleted/kept in batches.

executive.whatItDoes: Chravel is a collaborative trip-planning and coordination platform. A group creates a trip, then chats, splits expenses, syncs calendars, uploads media, imports reservations from email/PDF, and asks an AI concierge to plan and book — across web, installable PWA, and an iOS app that wraps the same web code.
whoItServes: Consumer travel groups (friends, family) — open, collaborative permissions
Pro / touring & business coordinators — role-based permissions
primaryFlows: Sign up / log in (Supabase Auth, email + Google OAuth) and hydrate session
Create a trip, invite members, resolve role/tier
Group chat + channels + broadcasts (Stream + Supabase Realtime)
Split expenses / manage subscriptions (Stripe web, RevenueCat iOS)
Ask the AI Concierge to plan, recommend, or take actions (text + voice)
Sync Google Calendar; import reservations via Smart Import
highestLeverage: src/integrations/supabase/client.ts — the single data gateway every read/write flows through
src/hooks/useAuth.tsx — gates the entire critical path; a bug here cascades everywhere
src/services/* (140 modules) — the de-facto API layer between UI and Supabase
supabase RLS policies (756) — the real security boundary, not the client
is_super_admin() + super_admins table — server-side privilege source of truth (gates audit-log reads and internal-admin routes)
admin_audit_logs (append-only + hash-chained) — tamper-evident record of every privileged action
src/lib/queryKeys.ts — cache-invalidation contract for all server state
topRisks: Auth hydration race → 'Trip Not Found' flash (the #1 documented regression)
Chat message loss on WebSocket reconnect (no replay/backfill in some paths)
Client-side permission checks diverging from RLS (privilege-escalation surface)
God files (Index.tsx, useTripTasks, useAuth, TripChat) concentrate risk and merge conflicts
Bundle weight (exceljs/pdf/charts/native chunks) degrades mobile first paint
topCleanups: Triage the 210 knip-flagged unused files — delete confirmed-dead, label framework entry points (api/, remotion/, scripts/) as Future/Unclear
Delete confirmed-dead exports/types after manual verification (knip: 386 exports, 433 types)
Reduce ~450 `any` usages at Supabase boundaries by adopting generated Database types
Lazy-load heavy chunks (exceljs, pdf/jsPDF via exportPdfClient.ts, recharts/charts, native) per tech_debt_report
Split static content god files (src/lib/blog.ts ~1.5k, mock datasets) out of the logic bundle
topMissing: Tests for chat reconnect/backfill, read receipts, and integration idempotency (TEST_GAPS.md)
Strong typing at boundaries — ~450 `any` usages remain
State machine for Smart Import (partial-import rollback / UX surfacing)
A single canonical mock/demo-data boundary separate from live service code

systemMap.name: Authentication & Session
systemMap.purpose: Session hydration, user identity, role resolution. Gates everything downstream.
actions: Split useAuth.tsx (1,307 LOC) into session/identity/role hooks
systemMap.failureModes: Hydration race → Trip Not Found flash; token refresh failure; demo/auth crossover

systemMap.name: Trip Management & Membership
systemMap.purpose: Trip CRUD, members, invites, archival. Trip context gates most features.
actions: Ensure existence vs membership checks are distinct (RLS + hook)
systemMap.failureModes: Access without membership; status drift; invite expiry

systemMap.name: Unified Messaging (Chat / Channels / Broadcasts)
systemMap.purpose: Trip chat, role channels, broadcasts, threads, reactions, read receipts.
actions: Honor eslint ban on legacy chatService in Stream surfaces
systemMap.failureModes: Message loss on reconnect; read-receipt write amplification; reaction refetch storm

systemMap.name: AI Concierge (Text + Voice)
systemMap.purpose: Gemini-powered assistant; text chat + Gemini Live voice; tool-calling that can mutate trip data.
actions: Keep AIConciergeChat.tsx modular (now ~510 LOC, decomposed from ~2.5k)
Route all writes through pending-actions buffer
systemMap.failureModes: Prompt injection; tool-call hallucination writing shared state; voice WebSocket drop; audio stuck after session end

systemMap.name: Calendar & Events
systemMap.purpose: Google Calendar bi-directional sync, event CRUD, RSVP, reminders.
actions: Keep sync idempotent (dedupe by external event id)
Cover sync-token expiry + recurring edge cases
systemMap.failureModes: Sync token expiry; bi-directional conflict; duplicate events from replay; timezone/recurring edge cases

systemMap.name: Payments & Subscriptions
systemMap.purpose: Consumer/Pro subscriptions, expense splitting, payment requests, balances.
actions: Model splits as state machines with optimistic locking
systemMap.failureModes: Webhook delivery failure; SDK mismatch per platform; cache drift; client-side billing bypass attempts

systemMap.name: Smart Import (Gmail / PDF / Artifacts)
systemMap.purpose: AI-powered import of trip data from emails, PDFs, links.
actions: Persist partial state on failure (never drop parsed items)
systemMap.failureModes: OAuth refresh failure; parser ambiguity → duplicates; partial import with no rollback

systemMap.name: Media Gallery & Storage
systemMap.purpose: Photo/video upload, compression, AI tagging, lightbox.
actions: Server-side validation + signed URLs + cleanup triggers
systemMap.failureModes: Upload timeout; quota exceeded; compression failure; tagging errors ignored

systemMap.name: Maps & Places
systemMap.purpose: Google Maps display, place search, autocomplete, location sharing.
systemMap.failureModes: Multiple map instances; key rate limiting; stale cache; listener leak

systemMap.name: Notifications & Realtime
systemMap.purpose: Push, email, SMS, in-app alerts, read receipts, badge counts.
systemMap.failureModes: Dual-path duplicate generation; badge drift; multi-device read desync; quiet-hours bypass

systemMap.name: Organizations & Teams (B2B)
systemMap.purpose: Seat-based billing, team management, org admin dashboard.
systemMap.failureModes: Seat billing desync; role inheritance ambiguity; separate invite flow

systemMap.name: Permissions & Roles
systemMap.purpose: Role-based access across consumer/pro/event trip types.
actions: Flow roles DB → RLS → hook → UI; never trust client claims
Check trip type before granting mutation access
systemMap.failureModes: Client checks diverging from RLS; escalation via direct API; tier gate disabling features for real trips

systemMap.name: Data Access Layer (Supabase client + services)
systemMap.purpose: Single Supabase gateway; 140 service modules act as the API layer between UI and DB.
actions: Keep all Supabase access out of JSX
Separate mock/demo data from live services
systemMap.failureModes: Direct JSX calls bypassing the gateway; mock/live contamination in service layer

systemMap.name: State Management
systemMap.purpose: TanStack Query (server) + Zustand (client) + Realtime + feature flags.
actions: Keep client state in the single src/store/ (the duplicate src/stores/ has been removed)
systemMap.failureModes: Ad-hoc invalidation drift across query keys

systemMap.name: Mobile / PWA / Native (Capacitor)
systemMap.purpose: Same web app wrapped via Capacitor 8 for iOS + installable PWA; platform-conditional code.
systemMap.failureModes: Native plugins eagerly imported on web; PWA/native layout drift

systemMap.name: Edge Functions & Migrations
systemMap.purpose: 97 Deno edge functions (AI, webhooks, imports, notifications, auth-gated mutations) + 419 SQL migrations.
systemMap.failureModes: Missing-secret fail-open; webhook auth gaps; migration drift

files.plain: The one doorway to the database and login. Every read and write goes through here.
files.tech: Supabase singleton client; the only sanctioned data gateway (CLAUDE.md forbids Supabase calls in JSX).

files.plain: Controls who is logged in and what they can do. The most safety-critical file in the app.
files.tech: 1,307 LOC auth/session/role hook. Gates the entire critical path; hydration timing here causes the Trip-Not-Found flash.

files.plain: The AI assistant's chat screen. Recently slimmed down so it's far easier to change safely.
files.tech: ~510 LOC — decomposed from the ~2,500-LOC 'god component' that was tech_debt_report.md #1. That top maintainability risk is now resolved; logic extracted into hooks/sub-components.
files.action: keep modular — guard against re-accreting responsibilities

files.plain: The trip chat screen. Big and re-renders more than it should.
files.tech: 1,262 LOC; tech_debt_report flags over-fetching/memoization. Stream-enabled surface (eslint bans legacy chatService import).
files.action: refactor — split render fns & filter tabs

files.plain: Wires the chat to the Stream messaging service.
files.tech: 1,202 LOC transport hook for Stream Chat; reconnect/backfill correctness is critical (TEST_GAPS.md).

files.plain: Generates fake 'demo' data. Risk of demo data leaking into real trips.
files.tech: 1,313 LOC; tech_debt_report flags unclear mock/live isolation. Demo-mode contamination is a zero-tolerance regression.
files.action: isolate behind a hard boundary; test no-leak invariant

files.plain: Large bank of trip mock data.
files.tech: 1,611 LOC of mock data inside the service layer (should not sit beside live services).
files.action: move to src/mockData/, gate behind dev/demo flag

files.plain: Auto-generated description of the database shape.
files.tech: 5,817 LOC generated types. tech_debt_report warns it may contain manual edits/bloat.

files.plain: The approved single source for assembling a trip's context for the AI.
files.tech: Canonical replacement for the deprecated enhancedTripContextService (enforced via eslint no-restricted-imports).

files.plain: Old version of the trip-context builder, officially retired.
files.tech: Deprecated; eslint blocks all import paths to it in favor of TripContextAggregator.

files.plain: Naming system for cached data so updates refresh the right screens.
files.tech: TanStack Query cache-key factory; the invalidation contract for server state.
files.action: keep; route all invalidation through it

files.plain: The landing/home page — heavy for a first screen.
files.tech: 1,529 LOC; tech_debt_report flags it as too heavy for first paint (~260K chunk).

files.plain: The marketing blog's content, stored as data — not real program logic.
files.tech: 1,475 LOC static BlogPost registry (source of truth for /blog and /blog/:slug). Data-heavy, low logic; large but inert. New entrant to the god-file list since the last refresh.
files.action: consider per-post files or MDX so the logic bundle stays lean

files.plain: Generates trip PDFs in the browser when the server export isn't available.
files.tech: 1,152 LOC client-side jsPDF fallback (file-level eslint-disable for no-explicit-any). New 1k+ entrant; pulls jsPDF/autotable into the bundle — a lazy-load + decomposition candidate.
files.action: lazy-load on export intent; split helpers; type the any surfaces

files.plain: Stray helper/scratch files that used to sit in the project root — now removed.
files.tech: Resolved: the Python patch scripts (in a declared 'No Python' repo) plus scratch JS/markdown at root have been deleted from the tree.

dependencies.notes: Coupling concentrates in three places: the Supabase client (imported almost everywhere — intentional gateway), the service layer (140 modules acting as the API), and a handful of god files. No formal cycle scan was run this session; treat cycle claims as Low confidence pending a madge/dpdm run.

hubs.role: Cache-key hub for all server state
Type hub for all typed queries

dependencies.orphansNote: knip lists current knip 'unused files', but many are framework entry points it cannot resolve: Vercel api/*.ts, Remotion compositions under remotion/, and scripts/*. These are Future/Unclear, not confirmed dead. Verify per-file before any deletion.

features.name: Trips & Membership
features.plain: Create trips, invite people, manage who's in.
fixes: DONE: membership state machine + access-guard tests (existence != access)

features.name: Unified Messaging
fixes: DONE: backfillMissedMessages on reconnect/visibility + reconnect tests; offline queue via Stream SDK + idempotency keys

features.name: AI Concierge
features.plain: AI helper that plans trips and can take actions, by text or voice.
missingStates: full-duplex Gemini Live voice not yet client-wired (dictation path only)
risks: prompt injection (mitigated: tool writes gated by pending-actions buffer)
fixes: DONE: pending-actions buffer covers every write tool (coverage test) + AIConciergeChat decomposed (~507 LOC) + dictation lifecycle cleanup; TODO: wire full-duplex voice over LiveKit

features.name: Payments & Subscriptions
risks: platform SDK boundary (Stripe web / RevenueCat iOS) must not mix
fixes: DONE: server-side entitlement (user_entitlements source of truth) + ops-layer webhook failure surfacing (failures table + dashboard view + replay script); TODO: richer split state machine (pending/confirmed/settled per participant)

features.name: Calendar & Events
features.plain: Shared calendar that syncs with Google.
missingStates: mount Google sync UI (reauth_required state ready, not yet wired)
fixes: DONE: idempotent dedupe by immutable external-id correlation (calendar_event_mappings, tested) + sync-token-expiry reauth UX state

features.name: Smart Import
features.plain: Pulls reservations out of emails and PDFs automatically.
missingStates: progress-phase indicator on PDF/URL path (Gmail path done)
fixes: DONE: checkpoint state machine + durable checkpoints + per-artifact apply_status (partial-state/rollback) + fingerprint dedupe + ImportPhaseIndicator progress UX (Gmail)

features.name: Media Gallery
risks: Gemini auto-tagging is best-effort (failures are non-blocking)
fixes: DONE: orphan-variant cleanup function + quota-exceeded upload guard (blocks before silent failure, tested)

features.name: Notifications
features.plain: Push, email, SMS, and in-app alerts.
fixes: DONE: race-safe dedupe (stream_message_id unique index) + badge reconcile on reconnect; per-user channel scoping is correct for the global feed (documented in useNotificationRealtime)

Routes wait for hydrated session before fetching

failurePoints: Fetch fires before hydration → Trip Not Found flash

failurePoints: Existence ≠ access: must check membership, not just row exists

flows.name: Send a chat message

steps.detail: Supabase Realtime for system messages, filtered by trip_id

failurePoints: WebSocket reconnect without backfill loses messages

flows.name: AI Concierge action

steps.detail: Confirm handler applies through normal mutation path

failurePoints: Missing confirm-handler case → confirm appears to work but writes nothing

flows.name: Subscribe / pay
failurePoints: Webhook delivery failure leaves state stale

flows.name: Smart Import a reservation
failurePoints: Retry drops parsed items if partial state not persisted

backend.summary: ~96 Deno edge functions handle AI, webhooks, imports, notifications, and auth-gated mutations; 378 SQL migrations define schema; 756 RLS policies are the real authorization boundary.

items.name: _shared/requireAuth.ts
items.purpose: Auth guard imported by every function
items.risk: Bypass if a function forgets to call it

items.name: _shared/validateSecrets.ts (requireSecrets)
items.purpose: Fail-fast on missing secrets at startup
items.risk: Fail-open documented for some guards (DEBUG_PATTERNS: CronGuard, capability token)

integrations.name: Supabase
integrations.hardening: RLS on every table; never expose service-role key client-side

integrations.name: Gemini / Vertex AI
integrations.hardening: Feature-flag kill switch; treat tool calls as untrusted (pending-actions buffer)

integrations.name: Stream Chat (GetStream)
integrations.hardening: Mint tokens server-side only; never ship secret to client

integrations.name: Stripe
integrations.failureModes: Web checkout down (RevenueCat iOS still works)

integrations.name: RevenueCat

integrations.name: Google Maps / Places
integrations.hardening: Restrict key by referrer; single map instance; cache places

integrations.name: Gmail API
integrations.hardening: Persist partial state; refresh tokens safely

integrations.name: Capacitor 8

glossary.plain: A reusable piece of screen logic (e.g. 'are we logged in?').
glossary.why: Most Chravel behavior lives in hooks; a buggy shared hook breaks many screens at once.

glossary.plain: A visual building block of the UI.
glossary.why: 354 of them; oversized ones (god components) are slow to change and risky.

glossary.plain: Data the app is currently holding (who's logged in, the open trip).
glossary.why: Server state (TanStack Query) vs client state (Zustand) must not drift.

glossary.plain: Small backend program that runs on demand (AI, webhooks, imports).
glossary.why: 97 of them are where trust-sensitive work happens; each must check auth + secrets.

glossary.plain: Database rules deciding which rows a user may see/change.
glossary.why: Chravel's real security boundary — 756 policies; the client cannot be trusted.

glossary.plain: A live feed pushing new messages/notifications instantly.
glossary.why: Must be filtered by trip and must replay missed data on reconnect, or messages are lost.

glossary.plain: A 'ping' from an outside service (e.g. Stripe) telling us something happened.
glossary.why: Payment/subscription truth arrives by webhook; a missed one leaves billing state stale.

glossary.plain: A versioned change to the database shape.
glossary.why: 419 of them; destructive ones need a two-phase plan to avoid data loss.
Media storage; needs validation, signed URLs, and cleanup.

glossary.plain: A named setting/secret provided at runtime (API keys).
glossary.why: Misconfigured/missing ones can fail open — a documented security risk here.

glossary.plain: An outside code package the app relies on.
glossary.why: Unused ones bloat the bundle and the security-audit surface; knip flagged 8 earlier but now reports 0 unused deps.

glossary.plain: Two files that need each other to load.
glossary.why: Causes subtle load-order bugs; worth scanning for in a follow-up.
Confuses humans and AI editors; safe to remove only after verification.

glossary.plain: Reorganizing code without changing what it does.
glossary.why: How god files get tamed — must be proven behavior-preserving with tests.

costEstimate.lens: What it would realistically cost to rebuild Chravel from scratch today — the same feature surface and quality bar, on the same managed platforms (Supabase, Stream, Stripe/RevenueCat, Google, Gemini/Vertex), by a competent team using current AI coding tools. Calibrated against how Chravel was actually built (a small team shipped it in roughly a year), not a worst-case hand-coded estimate. For internal reference, not a sales quote.
costEstimate.methodology: Bottom-up, then reality-checked. (1) Each major system is sized in engineering-hours for a competent team rebuilding a known spec — no product discovery, no pivots — using today’s AI coding tools. (2) Three effort bands: Conservative (lean, senior, heavy reuse, MVP-parity), Moderate (solid quality, tests on critical paths), High (enterprise rigor, broad tests, edge cases). (3) The whole table is calibrated to the actual build history — Chravel was shipped by a small team in roughly a year — so a known-spec rebuild lands in the same order of magnitude rather than the inflated multi-year figures naive estimators produce. (4) Hours convert to a schedule at ~6 productive hours/day, 5 days/week (30 hrs/week, ~1,500 hrs/year/dev). (5) Calendar time divides that effort across each vendor’s team size (freelancer = 1 dev solo, agency = 2 devs, high-end firm = 3 devs) with sub-linear efficiency (2 devs ≈ 1.8×, 3 devs ≈ 2.5×). (6) Cost = effort-hours × the vendor’s blended rate.
costEstimate.disclaimer: Realistic modern rebuild of a known spec on the same managed platforms, using current AI tooling — integration work, not platform engineering. Calibrated to the product’s actual ~1-year build. Excludes ongoing SaaS/infra, app-store fees, and post-launch maintenance.

model.patternNote: Effort is engineering-hours. Calendar time assumes ~6 productive (heads-down) hours inside an 8-hour workday — the remainder goes to standups, code review, planning, and context-switching — 5 days/week (30 hrs/week, ~1,500 productive hrs/year/dev). Adding developers is sub-linear: 2 devs ≈ 1.8× and 3 devs ≈ 2.5× one developer's throughput after coordination overhead, so calendar time falls less than headcount rises.

features.note: Scaffold, routing, Tailwind + shadcn/Radix, app shell, CI/CD
Supabase Auth, Google OAuth, role/tier resolution, hydration guards
Client gateway, ~140 service modules, query-key cache contract, generated types
Final schema, ~750 row-level-security policies as patterns, migrations
CRUD, invites, 3 trip tiers (consumer/pro/event), archival
Per-trip-type permission matrix, RLS-backed
Stream Chat, channels, broadcasts, threads, reactions, read receipts, reconnect/backfill
Gemini, ~38 tools, query classification, pending-actions write buffer
Vertex AI Live, audio session lifecycle
Bi-directional, idempotent, recurring events, RSVP
Stripe (web) + RevenueCat (iOS), webhooks, entitlements, expense splitting
Gmail OAuth, PDF/image parsing, Gemini extraction, checkpoint state machine
Upload, compression, AI tagging, lightbox, quota
Google Maps, places search, autocomplete, caching
Push/email/SMS (APNS/Resend/Twilio), dedup, badge counts
Seat-based billing, team management, org admin dashboard
Capacitor 8 iOS wrapper, offline, service worker, web/native parity
Append-only + hash-chained audit logs, super-admin, secret validation
Sentry, PostHog, runtime kill switches
Unit + integration across critical paths (~350 specs)
Landing, blog content system, SEO
Cross-feature wiring, bug-fixing, coordination

vendors.blurb: One developer working alone. Cheaper and less vetted; more management overhead, wider quality variance, and the longest calendar time.
Two dedicated devs (vetted senior + mid) with a PM — the most representative real-world option.
Three simultaneous senior devs at a top-tier firm with strong process, guarantees, and the fastest delivery.

assumptions: Assumes a competent team using today’s standard AI coding tools — the realistic 2026 default, not a hand-coded build.
Calibrated to reality: Chravel was actually built by a small team in roughly a year, so a known-spec rebuild is anchored to that order of magnitude.
Effort is engineering-hours; the schedule assumes ~6 productive (heads-down) hours inside an 8-hour workday, 5 days/week — 30 hrs/week, ~1,500 productive hrs/year per developer.
Team size by vendor: a freelancer works solo (1 dev), an established agency runs 2 dedicated devs, and a high-end firm runs 3 simultaneous devs.
Adding developers is sub-linear: 2 devs ≈ 1.8× and 3 devs ≈ 2.5× a solo developer’s pace after coordination overhead, so calendar time drops less than headcount rises.
US-market blended contractor rates (2026); offshore teams would be materially cheaper.
Rebuilding a known spec — no product discovery, design iteration, or pivots (those consumed much of the original calendar time and don’t recur).
Same managed platforms reused (Supabase, Stream, Stripe/RevenueCat, Google, Gemini) — you integrate them, you don’t rebuild auth servers, realtime, chat, or payment rails.
whyNotMore: Calibrated to the actual build: this product was shipped by a small team in about a year, so the rebuild is anchored to reality, not a theoretical worst case.
Modern AI coding tools do a large share of the boilerplate, wiring, and test scaffolding — the 2026 baseline, not optional.
Recreating a known spec skips discovery, dead-ends, and pivots — much of the original calendar time never recurs.
Off-the-shelf platforms do the heavy lifting: no custom auth server, realtime engine, chat backend, or payment processor to build.
UI is assembled from shadcn/Radix + Tailwind primitives rather than hand-built components.
File count is not effort: many of the 1,180 source files are small, repetitive, AI-generatable hooks and components.
costEstimate.note: A hand-coded build with no AI assistance would run roughly 1.7–2× these hours — a useful upper bound, but not today’s reality. Every input is adjustable in curated.json → costEstimate (per-system hours, rates, productive hours/day, team size, efficiency factors); all tables recompute. Rates are US-market blended (2026); offshore would be materially lower.
